revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
Ancient Egyptian Day]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25 |NAME-IN-CARTOUCHE = ja |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25-S29
Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|qq=}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|qq=}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|qq=}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|qq=}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|qq=}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|qq=}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|qq=}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|qq=}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|qq=}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|qq=}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|qq=}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|qq=}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|qq=}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|qq=}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sechet-hetep]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sechet-hetep]][[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sechet-hetep]]}} [[sn:Sechet-hetep]] [[de:Sechet-hetep]] [[sn:echet-hetep]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Egyptian mythology]]

The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sechet-hetep]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sechet-hetep]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sechet-hetep]]}} [[sn:Sechet-hetep]] [[de:Sechet-hetep]] [[sn:echet-hetep]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]]

Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' revision.text:
Ancient Egyptian Day]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25 |NAME-IN-CARTOUCHE = ja |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25-S29
Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}}

Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
[[de:Sechet-hetep]] Ancient Egyptian Day]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25 |NAME-IN-CARTOUCHE = ja |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25-S29
Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}}

The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}}

Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[de:Sechet-hetep]] Ancient Egyptian Day]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25 |NAME-IN-CARTOUCHE = ja |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25-S29
Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

revision.comment: Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
revision.text:
[[de:Sechet-hetep]] [[de:Sechet-hetep]] [[de:Sechet-hetep]] Ancient Egyptian Day]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25 |NAME-IN-CARTOUCHE = ja |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] |NAME2 = M20-R4:X1-N35:N25-S29
Sechet-hetep
''Sḫt-ḥtp''
''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]] of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * [[:en:Flooding of the Nile|Nilschwemme]]: Agricultural fields directly along the [[:en:Nile|Nil]], which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004}}gebiete: Tracts of land with [[:en:Reed bed|Röhricht]], occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * [[:en:Desert|Wüste]]nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099}}, [[:en:Oasis|Oase]], and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the [[:en:Wet season|Regenzeit]]. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993|1=Gefilde}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194|1=Suggestion}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Nilschwemme|1=Flooding of the Nile}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nil|1=Nile}}, which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902|1=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004|1=Schilf}}gebiete: Tracts of land with {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=Röhricht|1=Reed bed}}, occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * {{ill|Desert|en|Desert|WD=Q8514|lt=Wüste|1=Desert}}nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099|1=stream pool}}, {{ill|Oasis|en|Oasis|WD=Q43742|lt=Oase|1=Oasis}}, and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869|1=Field (agriculture)}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the {{ill|Wet season|en|Wet season|WD=Q3117517|lt=Regenzeit|1=Wet season}}. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}, wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582|1=Sechet-tjau}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119|1=Merencha}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633|1=Elmar Edel}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.'' In: ''News of the Academy of Sciences in Göttingen (NAWG)'' No. 8, Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen 1961, p. 216. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q91239|1=Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Notions in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|en|Bertha Porter|WD=Q16744842|lt=Bertha Porter|1=Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind L. B. Moss|ja|Rosalind Moss|lt=Rosalind L. B. Moss|lt_ja=Rosalind Moss|lt_de=Rosalind L. B. Moss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q15130156|1=Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings.'' Volume 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. {{translated page|de|Sechet-hetep|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Sechet-hetep]] {{wikidata link|Q2263577}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. ''Regions of the Offering'' } '''Sechet-hetep''' (also known as '''{{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993|1=Gefilde}} of the {{ill|Sacrifice (Religion)|de|Opfergabe|ja|Opfer (Religion)|lt=offering|lt_ja=Opfer (Religion)|lt_de=Opfergabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacrifice (Religion)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q179723|1=Sacrifice}}; abbreviated: '''Hetep''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|de|ägyptischen Mythologie|ja|Ägyptische Mythologie|lt=Egyptian mythology|lt_ja=Ägyptische Mythologie|lt_de=ägyptischen Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, a part of the region {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|WD=Q618140|lt=Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru}} of Osiris in the land {{ill|Ta-djeser|de|Ta-djeser|ja|Ta-djeser|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1631231|1=Ta-djeser}} (Realm of the Dead) and designated—as a brightly illuminated region—the place of provision. == Definition of the Term == The term Sechet-hetep consists of two independent word meanings. Due to various possible interpretations, no unambiguous assignment can be made. After death, Sechet-hetep not only served the sole purpose of enabling the deceased to have ''{{ill|eternal life|de|ein Leben im Paradies|ja|ewiges Leben|lt=a life in paradise|lt_ja=ewiges Leben|lt_de=ein Leben im Paradies|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eternal life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}'', but also encompassed other mythical aspects. A literal translation must therefore remain open, as the term Sechet-hetep was applicable in a very multifaceted manner. === Sechet === The term Sechet stands for field or, more broadly, {{ill|Landscape|de|Gefilde|ja|Landschaft|lt=regions|lt_ja=Landschaft|lt_de=Gefilde|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Landscape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1497993|1=Gefilde}}, which implies a certain area and can therefore also be rendered as “landscape.” An interpretation in the sense of “land” as in “field” or “arable land” is not appropriate, as it would {{ill|suggestion|de|suggerieren|ja|Suggestion|lt=suggest|lt_ja=Suggestion|lt_de=suggerieren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/suggestion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q690194|1=Suggestion}} an area that is too small. The term Sechet referred to several types of landscapes: * {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|WD=Q1519552|lt=Nilschwemme|1=Flooding of the Nile}}: Agricultural fields directly along the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|WD=Q3392|lt=Nil|1=Nile}}, which were flooded by the annual Nile inundation. * {{ill|Insel|de|Nilinseln|ja|Insel|lt=Isle|lt_ja=Insel|lt_de=Nilinseln|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: Landmasses in the Nile above the {{ill|Wasserspiegel|de|Wasserspiegel|ja|Wasserspiegel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wasserspiegel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105221902|1=Q105221902}} that could be used as enclosed areas for agriculture. * {{ill|Schilfrohr|de|Schilf|ja|Schilfrohr|lt=Schilf|lt_ja=Schilfrohr|lt_de=Schilf|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schilfrohr|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q11186004|1=Schilf}}gebiete: Tracts of land with {{ill|Reed bed|en|Reed bed|WD=Q620424|lt=Röhricht|1=Reed bed}}, occurring in stagnant or slowly flowing waters up to one meter deep. * {{ill|Desert|en|Desert|WD=Q8514|lt=Wüste|1=Desert}}nrandgebiete: Peripheral desert areas comprising {{ill|Tümpel|de|Tümpel|ja|Tümpel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tümpel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q291099|1=stream pool}}, {{ill|Oasis|en|Oasis|WD=Q43742|lt=Oase|1=Oasis}}, and only temporarily usable {{ill|Kulturboden|de|Ackerboden|ja|Kulturboden|lt=arable land|lt_ja=Kulturboden|lt_de=Ackerboden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kulturboden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q188869|1=Field (agriculture)}}, which gradually dry out after the end of the {{ill|Wet season|en|Wet season|WD=Q3117517|lt=Regenzeit|1=Wet season}}. === Hetep === Hetep is understood, in relation to the Sechet-iaru region, as a sub-area of the {{ill|Opfermahl|de|Opfermahl|ja|Opfermahl|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Opfermahl|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}. The deceased, upon entering the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|WD=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}, wished to receive the divine offering due to them—a process associated with their ascension to an independent deity after arriving in the Duat. == See Also == * {{ill|Sechet-tjau|de|Sechet-tjau|ja|Sechet-tjau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q2263582|1=Sechet-tjau}} * {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|ja|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q1571119|1=Merencha}} == Literature == * {{ill|Elmar Edel|de|Elmar Edel|ja|Elmar Edel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elmar Edel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q105633|1=Elmar Edel}}: ''On the Inscriptions on the Seasonal Reliefs of the "World Chamber" from the Sun Sanctuary of Niuserre.''
